message: 🌱 Add kured: improve security of reboot mechanism mission (#683)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kured-416-improve-security-of-reboot-mechanism",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kured: improve security of reboot mechanism",
+    "description": "improve security of reboot mechanism. This issue affects 6+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kured troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kured deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured -n kured --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kured configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kured configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured\nkubectl get configmap -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kured\n```\nHello,\n\nWe would really like to use kured but there is reluctance about a fully privileged daemonset that has access to execute arbitrary commands as root on every node.  I have reviewed some options that could allow rebooting more securely."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for improve security of reboot mechanism",
+        "description": "Current state: I've also implemented the first two todos locally and tested it in my home-cluster. The signal in general reboots the server (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS) gracefully, that looks good. However, with this securityContext the command errors with \"permission denied\":\n```yaml\n    securityContext:\n  \n```yaml\n{{- if .Values.configuration.rebootSignal}}\n  privileged: false\n  allowedCapabilities: ['KILL']\n{{- else }}\n  privileged: true\n  allowedCapabilities: ['*']\n{{ end }}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm improve security of reboot mechanism is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured -n kured --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n kured --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Current state: I've also implemented the first two todos locally and tested it in my home-cluster. The signal in general reboots the server (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS) gracefully, that looks good.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{{- if .Values.configuration.rebootSignal}}\n  privileged: false\n  allowedCapabilities: ['KILL']\n{{- else }}\n  privileged: true\n  allowedCapabilities: ['*']\n{{ end }}",
+        "Jul 28 22:33:46 el8-test.novalocal systemd[1]: Received SIGRTMIN+5 from PID 1764 (n/a).\n...\nJul 28 22:33:47 el8-test.novalocal systemd[1]: Reached target Shutdown.",
+        "securityContext:\n      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false\n      capabilities:\n        add:\n        - CAP_KILL\n        drop:\n        - '*'\n      privileged: false"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kured",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kured"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Daemonset",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kubereboot/kured/issues/416",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubereboot/kured"
+    },
+    "reactions": 6,
+    "comments": 29,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kured installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-11T06:35:14.384Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
